text.title: decomposition-react Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for decomposition-react. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: MUST organize directory structure as: src/components/ui/ (reusable primitives, 30-50 lines each), src/components/features/ (domain-specific features, 40-70 lines), src/hooks/ (cross-feature hooks), src/services/ (API clients, 50-70 lines), src/stores/ (state management, 40-60 lines), src/types/ (TypeScript type files), src/utils/ (utilities, 40-60 lines). Each component MUST have max 80 lines; tests and stories co-locate with the component.
rule.rationale: This layout matches feature-sliced design and Next.js conventions. Files stay readable on screen (30-80 lines). LLMs can load full files without truncation. Co-location (test + story + component in same folder) reduces imports and keeps related code visible together.

rule.statement: Components MUST be composed from smaller components: a `Dashboard` feature fetches data via `useDashboardData` hook, then renders subcomponents (`DashboardHeader`, `DashboardMetrics`, `DashboardChart`, `DashboardTable`). No component MUST exceed 80 lines; if a component exceeds this, split it before coding.
rule.rationale: Component composition is the only composable pattern in React. Splitting early prevents god components and trains agents to think in terms of interfaces, not monoliths. Subcomponents are private (in features/Dashboard/) unless needed elsewhere (promote to ui/); this enforces encapsulation.

rule.statement: Components MUST target 50-80 lines; absolute max 150 lines. Hooks MUST target 40-60 lines; absolute max 100 lines. Services MUST target 50-70 lines; absolute max 150 lines. Pages (Next.js) MUST target 50-70 lines; absolute max 150 lines. Server Actions MUST target 60-80 lines; absolute max 150 lines. Tests MUST target 100-150 lines; absolute max 300 lines.
rule.rationale: Files under 80 lines fit on a laptop screen without scrolling. LLMs with 4k-token context windows can load multiple 80-line files in parallel. Larger files require pagination or truncation, reducing reasoning quality and increasing the chance of missed edge cases. File size limits force modularity: small files are easier to test, understand, and reuse.

rule.statement: Custom hooks MUST extract stateful logic (useState + effects), API calls (useQuery, useFetch), or form state. Hooks SHOULD be 40-60 lines; if a hook exceeds 100 lines, split it or consider a state-management library. Hook names MUST start with `use` and describe the behavior (useDashboardData, useDashboardFilters, useDebounce).
rule.rationale: Reusable logic outside components is the core of React composition. Named hooks telegraph intent. Hooks under 100 lines are testable and reusable. Larger hooks become implicit state machines; use Zustand or TanStack Query instead.

rule.statement: MUST organize Next.js app as: app/ (page.tsx per route, layout.tsx for shared structure, _components/ for route-local components), lib/actions/ (Server Actions), lib/db/ (database layer), lib/validations/ (Zod schemas). Route-specific components live in _components/ (underscore prevents them being routes). Each page.tsx MUST be 40-60 lines.
rule.rationale: Next.js enforces file-based routing; the underscore convention hides internal components. Server Actions co-locate with the pages that call them. This layout keeps secrets (database, API keys) in lib/, not exposed in app/.

rule.statement: Components MUST target 50-80 lines; max 150. Hooks MUST target 40-60 lines; max 100. Services MUST target 50-70 lines; max 150. Pages (Next.js) MUST target 50-70 lines; max 150. Server Actions MUST target 60-80 lines; max 150. Tests MUST target 100-150 lines; max 300. If a file exceeds max, it is a refactoring candidate.
rule.rationale: Limits force modularity. Files fitting one screen (50-80 lines at 100 chars/line) are readable on laptop displays without scrolling. LLMs with 4k-token context windows can load multiple 80-line files in parallel. Larger files require truncation or pagination, reducing reasoning quality.
